purpose: Machine-readable description of the public curation hooks and the hidden replay axes. This file does not expose hidden data.

research_questions: Can quality and source filters improve fixed-budget small-LM training?
Can filters reject low-value text without collapsing domain diversity?
hidden_replay_axes: quality false positives and false negatives
invalid_patterns: using heldout text or hidden-shard signals for candidate selection
dropping most of the corpus to improve only the public proxy

research_questions: Can ordering or prioritization improve early-token learning?
Can ranking preserve useful rare sources while removing obvious junk?
hidden_replay_axes: domain balance under fixed token budget
invalid_patterns: using random order without recorded seeds

research_questions: Can near-duplicate removal improve effective token diversity?
Can dedup avoid deleting legitimate repeated technical patterns?
invalid_patterns: removing documents only because they appear in the public fixture
reporting dedup rate without selection hashes

research_questions: Can curriculum schedules improve loss at the same token budget?
Can easy-to-hard or mixture schedules survive hidden seed replay?
invalid_patterns: choosing order from hidden loss feedback
using wall-clock speed as the primary quality metric
claiming public proxy movement as hidden training evidence

official_replay_axes.description: Apply the submitted hooks to hidden and public corpus shards before fixed tiny-LM training.
Promote only when the median hidden loss improves the locked baseline under the required seed policy.
Top promoted candidates are rerun at a larger token or model budget before receiving a scaled label.
